plan_adherence_notes: The branch did execute the main verification steps from the plan: the runtime baseline matched the research assumptions (Node v22.22.1, pnpm 10.30.1), `pnpm --dir frontend install` completed in this worktree without lockfile drift, and `pnpm --dir frontend test` passed with 9 test files and 54 tests. The execute diff did not stay within the plan, though. Instead of only capturing the baseline, running install/test, and reporting results, it also changed application and test code (`frontend/src/app/router.tsx`, `frontend/src/app/providers.tsx`, `frontend/src/app/routerFuture.ts`, `frontend/src/lib/query/hooks.ts`, `frontend/src/__tests__/helpers.tsx`, `frontend/src/__tests__/MarketDetail.test.tsx`) and committed generated build outputs (`frontend/dist/*`, `frontend/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo`). Those edits were not described in the four planned steps.
missed_questions: If the full frontend suite already passes but emits warnings, is warning cleanup in scope for this task, or should execution stop at reporting the reproduction?
Is a task titled 'verify frontend test suite runs in worktree' allowed to change production code and test fixtures, or must it be limited to install/test/reporting work?
If a non-blocking `pnpm --dir frontend build` check is run, should generated artifacts such as `frontend/dist/` and `frontend/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo` be left out of the branch?
reason: The frontend suite does run in this worktree: `pnpm --dir frontend install` was clean and `pnpm --dir frontend test` passed with 9 files and 54 tests on April 11, 2026. Changes are still requested because execution broadened a verification task into unplanned source-code and generated-artifact changes, so the branch does not cleanly demonstrate plan-following validation-only work.
